content:
While there were a lot of good changes proposed for 2.5.1, the lack of qol improvements for a couple of particularly crappy enlightens is a bit disappointing.

Helot's E1, for example, puts a card into your hand at the beginning of every fight that essentially does nothing except force you to either pay 1 arith or shuffle an unwanted card into your deck if you want to remove it from your hand, which is extremely annoying in Gelot teams where hand space is at a premium.

An easy fix would be to simply make the generated card Fleeting, since there's no reason why you'd actually want to shuffle the created card into your deck anyway.
(Edit: Apparently some Mouchette players actually do that. Maybe Helot could get a small buff in the form of -1 cost to the created card <:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184> ? Or just a 5th option that destroys the card.)

Jenkin's E2 is another good example. It may have made sense early on in the game's lifecycle when Jenkin could consistently function as a team's main damage dealer, but now it often ends up discarding key damage or survivability cards from other characters, undercutting Jenkin's ability to function as a situational support.

This one doesn't really have an obvious fix, but maybe it could draw the card nearest the top that belongs to Jenkin or something.
thread_title: Enlightens Should Never Make a Character Worse

content:
A lot of teams and realm mechanics use a lot of hand space: embryos, insights, retain cards, duplication, draw etc really need a lot of it.
This constrains are why the +2 hand space wheels are so important, but even then is some time not enough, so for people that don't have them it can be pretty hard to manage it depending on the team.
Would it be possible to increase the hand space by 1? The +2 hand wheel will still be very good, having 13 in place of 12 is still fantastic for the teams that already wanted it.

thread_title: Cursed relic option bug

content:
Pandia really does struggle at most points in the game! Designed for her exalt to scale off of counter yet providing barely any payoff! Let alone generating barely any counter in the first place! For scaling off of counter there are better options, and for generating counter there are also better options! She doesn't do either job well enough to stand out! Triggering some sort of effect when hit would certainly fit her character. Perhaps she could turn small portions of temporary counter into permanent counter every time she's hit? Would give her a much stronger niche in counter teams for stacking perm counter and synergizing with temporary counter... Or simply giving her good counter gen or synergy with other counter awakeners in the first place! There's a lot of different suggestions and routes to be done to help Pandia find her footing, she needs to focus on a specific role in her kit and do it well!!!
Pics related.
